reviews.review_text: Caravan park has a very relaxed feel with very friendly staff that are always there to help or just say hello when passing.|Have stayed there many times and will definitely return

Reflections tuncurry never disappoints, has a great atmosphere & are given updates on what’s happening in the park via text message which I love.. walking distance the Rockpool or beach, shops, club’s & restaurants

Horrible customer service by management, area manager!
Do not book here unless you have to cancel due to changed circumstances! They keep your money, do not refund you because you’re out of their (60 day notice) how ridiculous! Considering we booked this 3 weeks before so we had no 60 days notice to even cancel.
Please be super aware of this caravan park! I’ve heard of other families have the same issue, as you can see below there’s a lot of similar reviews. Absolutely disgusting behaviour.
Reception was friendly and accomodating. The place is close to the centre and Rock pool cafe was 3mins drives. Shower was great
Love this caravan park 😍

This Park is Very Very tired! My spouse and I stay yearly for four weeks on Site H14 and we believe you have a five star location however it is degrading to a 2 star park!  We consider the staff are friendly, professional and doing what they can to keep the Park in a semblance of order. We have mentioned in the past our site is not level and it is difficult to manoeuvre onto that small slab site. We have to use levelling ramps on the offside of our caravan and have to double jack the caravan drawer bar to get it all level. The site may look level however it is not. Drainage is an issue and other sites within the park are below average. The ammenities at the Park are below average, the toilet seats do not align, some are jagged and the general overall facilities are well past their use by date and in need of renovation. We have been to other Reflection Parks that are updating and renovating but do not see much going on at Tuncurry and wonder why? The pricing rates are similar too? I believe there needs a lot of further research and development planning, comparisons with other parks planning and staged implementation to update this OLD Park.
Great double concrete site. Amenities good. Located next to great beach and swimming enclosure (Rock Pool beach)

The park is extremely well located close to beach and shopping however it is in very poor condition. The amenities are very old and the sites are dusty and have very poor amenity. Fortunately the staff are fine but the operator Reflections have no concept of a modern caravan parks.
Needs a lot of tlc

Booked a standard cabin. Place had black mould on windows sills and roof. Lovely rubbish smell as bins were right across from bedroom window. Toilet broken constantly running couldn't flush without risk of overflow. Previously we were sent to wrong location in park and without key. Called office staff and were told they were fully booked and couldn't offer us anything else, so we didn't stay as it wasn't safe for us or our 4 month old. Just went home. Hoping to get a refund
A great spot, a beach side fishing and boating paradise, so much to do and explore.

poor treatment by staff, extremely rude and inconsiderate, made me push my tinny into the park instead of letting another car in to quickly drop it off mind you i have a extremely injured knee. facility’s looked like something out of a horror movie, camp kitchen fridge full off rotting bait, toilets had human faeces all over the floors including in the showers, blocked toilets and was all left a mess for over
24 hours, not a place i would recommend taking children.
Great location. The young lady of the reception was really helpful and informative.
Nice spot right on the beach and close to town, shops, restaurants etc. Very quiet time of year. Super modern and clean amenities block and camp kitchens. 5 star amenities.

The best accommodation for us every year for our Bowls Carnival.
Lovely clean cabins,friendly staff and what a view and location.

Lovely clean park with good amenities in the cabins and camp kitchen/toilets. Good value for being a few minutes walk to the beach. Walkable into town for good restaurants and shops, although a big grocery store is further away. It was quiet when we were there despite being school holidays. Would return again

New staff and management are amazing, I think the best we have ever had in coming here 3 times a year for 33 years. Sherri is so lovely, even popping around on a regular basis to check in with us. Honestly yes the park needs a little work, but I see how things are changing and how much the staff are doing to make this happen.
Best park ever. Plenty of shade. We had ocean view, which probably helped. And only a short walk to beach. Not patrolled though. Good amenities and plenty of camp kitchens.

impeccably clean, facilities well priced and everything for a unit included, staff friendly and attentive and the grounds well maintained.  you couldn't ask for more from a caravan, camping and cabin park (and i almost never write reviews). thankyou
So close to the beach with a great local cafe at the beach

A nice park though some facilities quite dated. Was nice and close to the beach but the park had too many codes to remember, One for  entry and exit via boom gate, one for entry and exit via pedestrians access , and one for the ablution blocks, if I forgot my phone I would be locked out. Also the park was at capacity and hence the showers were  often lukewarm.
Wifi needs fixing no excuse for shit Wifi these days

Booked a site in April 2025 only to be told we had been moved to a different site when we arrived. No other sites available. Site we were given was too small for our van, was extremely difficult to access and had a terrible slope. Changing our site without consultation is unacceptable.

Booked over phone told them we’d be late checkin. No codes left. No site given. Couldn’t access at 7.00pm. Had our 1 year old with us. Had to wait until management turned up and allocated site and then by darkness was left putting up tent in howling wind with screaming 1 year old. Missed out on dinner at the restaurant.||Won’t be back. Do better.
Staying on a site with caravan.  Nice and tidy, amenities very clean . Close to local shops and not to far from the water

Stayed in a cabin and not impressed with the very late check in time of 3pm. Told us they would call us to try and get us in early. After no phone call we arrived at service desk at 3pm only for cabin to still not be ready. Then had to wait whilst a big line of people also checking in at this time jumped in front of us. After getting into our cabin late we were then immediately refused a request for late checkout saying cleaners needed access to clean our cabin at 10am. So on the day we arrived cleaners didn’t start cleaning to after 2pm but on the day we leave they had to clean at 10am? Very poor service. Facilities are run down too with temporary amenities still sitting in the middle of the park but haven’t been in use for years. Other amenities up near beach also still sitting in park locked up but not in use. Location near beach is fabulous but caravan park is run down and peak season is very expensive given the lack of facilities compared to other parks in the area (ie no playground, no pool, etc).
Sites are mostly dirt, staff are unhelpful.
It's close by the Forster beach, very nice view and can hear the sound of waves in the night times... Favourite for those who wants to stay long in the beach and kayak, SUP for fun

Nothing ruins a family holiday faster than waking up to stolen belongings — and being blamed for it.

We stayed at Reflections Caravan Park, Tuncurry after making a late booking over the phone and checking in late that night. We set up, locked the caravan, bikes and cars, and spent the evening with our son playing with his new RC car and charging the batteries so he could use it the next morning.

When we woke up, we discovered that RC car batteries, a power bank, and a fishing rod had been stolen overnight. After looking around and confirming they were gone, we spoke to the park attendant. Her first response was, “Did you lock it up?” — which was incredibly frustrating and unhelpful given the items stolen. How do you “lock up” loose items like batteries, a power bank, or a fishing rod? Take them into bed?

We felt upset and unsafe, and I asked for a refund for the night so we could leave. This was refused due to it being peak period, and I was told I had spoken rudely — despite being already stressed about having our belongings stolen.

I then contacted head office, who said they would get back to me as the situation was not acceptable. It has now been four days, I’ve followed up three times, and there has been no response from the regional manager.

Because we no longer felt safe or looked after, we chose to leave early to protect our family and avoid further theft. All we asked for was that one night’s accommodation refunded.

I will say the Reflections head office staff member was polite and tried to help, but the park manager was rude, abrupt, and dismissive. Over roughly $100, we were made to feel like money mattered more than guest safety.

Based on this experience, I wouldn’t recommend staying here, or at Reflections Caravan Parks generally, if you value security and customer care.
Amazing staff very helpful and always super friendly  - doggy friendly- amazing facilities for dogs. Will be back. Mr whippy and seafood man 🤩.

Just love this place. Been here since 2 January. This is peak season and yes it is busy, but the facilities have been clean all week. Very cheap, well serviced laundry. Outstanding management, with great activities for kids and adults organised. This evening there was live music, great singer and muscian with a takeaway pizza van on site. The location is second to none, next to beach and rock pool. Already booked for next year. Wonderful site. Highly recommend

Stayed here for 10 days in our van. Location was great. Amenities clean. Staff cheerful and helpful. We were here mid December and it was relatively quiet. Had a fabulous stay and would definitely stay again if I'm in the area.

**★☆☆☆☆ — Do Not Recommend**

I *strongly do not recommend* staying at this caravan park based on my experience. Unfortunately, what should have been a relaxing getaway turned into one of the most disappointing stays I’ve ever had.

**🚫 Cleanliness Issues**
The park’s facilities were poorly maintained and dirty throughout my stay. The amenities block — including showers and toilets — looked neglected and had not been cleaned properly, which immediately set a negative tone for our visit. Cleanliness is one of the most important things travellers look for in caravan parks, and this place failed badly in that regard. ([Park Talk][1])

**🛠️ Poor Maintenance**
Grounds were overgrown, rubbish bins were overflowing, and pathways were unkempt. It gave the impression the park doesn’t invest in basic upkeep or presentation, which made the whole place feel run-down rather than welcoming. ([Park Talk][2])

**📍 Facilities Fell Short**
Advertised amenities like BBQ areas, laundry and common spaces were either in bad condition or unavailable, and the Wi-Fi was unreliable. These are essentials for a comfortable stay, especially for longer visits. ([The Luxe Insider][3])

**🙄 Poor Customer Service**
When I raised concerns with staff, the response was dismissive and unhelpful. Rather than addressing the issues, I felt like my feedback was brushed aside.

**💰 Not Worth the Price**
Considering the state of the park and lack of service, the nightly rates were far too high. You’re better off choosing another caravan park nearby with better facilities and more attentive management.

Overall, this caravan park was disappointing on multiple levels from cleanliness and maintenance to service and facilities. I *will not be returning*, and I don’t recommend it to anyone expecting a pleasant, stress-free stay.

Stayed 3 nights in our van. Have stayed hete before and didn't disappoint.Sites easy to access.  Special shout to Leonie for makimg staying an extra night a easy process.
Well pleased
Beautiful spot.
Camped here. Great location

The cabin was lovely and clean. The crockery, cutlery, bedding, bath towels were all clean.  Coffee, tea and milk provided was great for the first few cups of beverages. Our location was close to family residing in Tuncurry, also being in walking distance to beach and clubs.  Good value for what you got as in clean everything in cabin, large size fridge, two ceramic stove tops, crockery, cutlery, cooking implements, table and four chairs, wardrobe, tv, two sets of roller blinds on each window, aircon, four seats outdoor setting with table, beautiful hand soap, shampoo and body lotion dispensers in bathroom. Noise level was great considering it was the school holidays and the kids didn't come out riding their bikes and skateboards until after 8am of a morning. Lovely peaceful atmosphere for having a short or long holiday break.  We will definitely stay there again.

worst experience with a campsite to date. They missplaced my booking, after driving 10hrs and arriving late at night (as mentioned in booking) rung out of hours and a real rude lady mocked me and then cancelled my booking leaving me stranded at 11pm. Was lost for words

Good location but you can only take one person in a car at a time, staff are not that friendly and the rooms are half camper trailer don’t stay here don’t stay here the only good thing was fishing spot.

Very close to the breakwall and all camping sites are powered. Make sure you get your electric extension wires. I camped here for a day and loved it. Excellent facilities and you even have EV car charging facilities inside the park. Wifi services also provided. Camping charges are a bit high but the location and other facilities may justify this. Will be back again.

Arrived and experienced a mechanical problem. Stayed one night at a concrete slab site before transferring into a cabin. Very helpful staff. Amenities coded and very clean. Communal campfire very welcomming. Basic cabin, acceptable but floors needed a good sweep and mop. Bed comfortable, very crisp sheets and soft towels. Found the extra blanket. Good shower size and excellent hot water. Great access to beach and river views. Lovely and quiet mid week. Recommended.
We are not in a cabin but they look good.
A bit outdated. Needs a revamp. Not worth the price paid on a powered site. Would rather stay at nrma down the road.

We rented a double slab caravan spot.
Amenities our first class.
Fire pit meet up at 4 pm
very short walk to a dog friendly beach.
Absolutely amazing, had Karen at front office who took very good care of me and my family and has a beautiful beach close by.

Awesome location in the heart of Forster.  2 mins walk to beach & restaurants.  Been many times prior but never at Xmas.   The new managers are lovely.   Super friendly & made checking in on Boxing day a breeze by having a check in desk with info pack as you drove in.  Nothing was to much trouble they are a great asset to the park.    A few activities for kids & they had an afternoon of live music which was great.  New amenities & camp kitchen was really nice.   Our caravan site was large & really level.   The park has location location location 👍

Very disappointing stay, no grass on our “grass” site with the exception of the 20cm tall grass under our van and random clumps of weeds throughout the dirt, site hadn’t been cleaned prior to arrival, rubbish and random rusted bits of metal throughout. Roads are terrible, pot holes throughout, nothing to do for young children I.e no playground, pool, water play etc etc. Won’t be coming back again

Great affordable little cabin , everything you need for a relaxing seaside stay close to beach & shops all in walking distance with lots to explore by short drives aswel . I will be back as Forster has the best Ocean pool to swim , I highly recommend to stay @ Reflections Forster !

We just stayed for one night but it's definitely worth staying more. The location is interesting. The camp site has everything you need and more. Very polite, clean and quiet. You can walk to a few restaurants in just a few minutes. We were told you can see dolphins there sometimes.

I just want to say its amazing that you guys helped out “Inside a mans mind” Justin Cumner and Norman the forman.

Havent stayed here but the fact you guys were willing to help is awesome.

Great work guys.

We've stayed here before, the location can't be faulted for walking to restaurants etc, great for those that just want to stroll out for breakfast. We're in a van so no review's on the cabins but they do look very well set-up. Xx

We arrived to find that our booking had been canceled without our knowledge, and the substantial deposit was not returned. They offered another cabin, but for 3 adults instead of 5. We thought they would give a total refund but they wanted to charge us more even though two of us had to find accommodation elsewhere in the town!

We booked a deluxe cabin for 4 nights in October 2025.||Pros:|- Location of the park, it's 250m walk to the beach, with local restaurants and cafes surrounding. |- Mid-week booking discount|- Clean and tidy|- Nespresso machine and pods included with cabin stay||Cons: |- No on-site facilities for families to enjoy (no pool, playground or games room etc). There are board games available to use but that's it. |- No internal laundry. Communal laundry is available at $7 a load for washing (excluding laundry products) and $8 a load for drying.|- Kitchenette makes meals tricky. However there is a communal BBQ if required.|- The WiFi connection is non-existent! We were given password to connect but the connection is extremely poor and kept dropping out. We had to hotspot to be able to use the TV for streaming services.||As someone with school aged kids, we have no plans to use Reflections Forster again. This park may be a better fit for adult friends or retirees travelling together.

Very disappointed in park They had 3 attempts to find a site that was suitable for our van park grounds are in extremely poor condition and the internal roads of the park are a disgrace and extremely dangerous to walk on wouldnt recommend or be returning a massive let down
Such a breath of fresh air when I needed a break. Thankyou staff were lovley.

Park has seen some upgrades to the bathroom facilities and are very nice. Really needs a pool, heated as per Hawks Nest. Cannot comment on staff as its all contact free, checking in online and getting access codes via email. Is close to everything Forster has to offer.

Our stay from 21 -27 September on a powered site was our third time at Forster Beach Holiday Park, Every time we holiday there its gets better each time. Facilities are first class staff are courteous and friendly it is just a great park. We checked out the Parks at Tuncurry but Forster Beach is the pick for us.
Love it we will be back.
Regards John & Narelle McGregor
Awesome

For the price of the premium cabins I was definitely expecting a little more luxury $650 for 2 nights and only had got the basics. Some improvements I’d like to mention would be:
* softer bed possibly larger as well very small double.
*condiments cupboard just basic one eg. oil etc to cook with as it did state fully equipped kitchen
* cleaning the exhaust fan as this had heaps of fluff and mould on it.
*also checking bathroom for ants as we had a bath then had 10 ants appear in the bathroom
*heater/Aircon in the bedroom.

The location of the park is great I enjoyed that aspect and the large carpark as we had a boat on and were able to park next to our cabin for I was disappointed in the quality of the room for the price I was paying. If your going to charge that amount the room would want to be upgraded a bit

They really sorted us out. We had a great stay here. Pricing was reasonable, location was amazing. The places had everything they needed. Awesome stay.
Don't get put off by the HQ people that answer the phones. The people at reception and that helped with our room were all amazing!
Great caravan park right on the beach. Has everything you need.

the lady at the front had the worst attitude i have ever experienced i was lost looking around and she had no help just being so rude i have never been somewhere with worse service in my life it is astonishing how someone can be so rude to another person i hope you have a long conversation with that girl i have no idea what her name was but oh my god please do some more training on your employees i am appalled to say the least i will not be coming back in the future

Always stay here for about 8 weeks a year in a caravan. Close to beach, Rockpool and Tuncurry Beach Bowlo. Handy to shops. Helpful staff if needed. Amenities are clean and passable, nice hot showers if you need it.
Usually lovely temperate weather in winter, but this year the weather let us down. Still enjoyed our stay.

Stayed 3 nights in a deluxe cabin.

Very clean, very comfortable. We usually stay in hotels, this was quite a pleasant change.

Loved the privacy and the public laundry amenities were great.

The bathroom and kitchen tiles were cracked and the vanity cabinet was water damaged, and verandas timber decking beginning to rot, so an upgrade in the near future would be recommended.

Our advice would be to install heating in the bedrooms (they are pretty cold this time of year).

Overall a good stay and would definitely stay again.

Absolutely top class accomodation and park itself. We stayed in deluxe cabin 8. Spotless top notch furniture and beds.The whole park had everything covered. This is the best caravan park we have stayed in. Informative and great staff.

We had a very pleasant stay, loved the short walk to coffee and shops, along with access to short walks along the breakwall with our little pouch.||Loved our double slab with our Motorhome, would have loved to have had access to an ensuite (didn’t see any).||All amenities were clean and hygienic ( I imagine in the more busier holiday times there would be more cleaning schedules throughout the day.||Would I return? MOST DEFINITELY!

Great caravan park with clean, bright amenities, dog wash & dog washing machine. You never know when you need to clean something for your pet & they usually don't offer a pet washing machine. Separate laundry facilities for your clothes. Great spot with big sites. We love it here

Stayed on a powered caravan site. Nice clean amenities, with a few different blocks which was nice to see in quite a large park.
Good access to outside the park, with an easy walk to the breakwall, beach, swimming spot and playground. Also easy walk to town and shops.
Would definitely stay again.

WARNING: I cancelled a booking to camp here due to severe weather warning issued by the BOM..Unfortunately Reflections Tuncurry have opted to keep my deposit with Andre repeatedly telling me “it’s policy”. I would like to think a legitimate cancelation would elicit a refund but unfortunately Reflections Tuncurry see this as an opportunity to make a money grab. When I subsequently enquired, Grace had the audacity to tell me to “take care in this weather” which is what I think I am doing by not driving 300km to camp next to a flood prone river during destructive winds and an extreme rain fall event. NOTE: you will not get your deposit back if you cancel, even if it is for legitimate safety reasons.
Very clean and comfortable units. Very clean amenities. Friendly and helpful staff. Quick and easy check in.

We stayed at Tuncurry reflections caravan park for the Easter long weekend in April 2025 and it was great! We had a double slab site (D2) We were close to the walk way to get to the rock pool. The park is huge with three amenities. The location was great and we will be back. Thanks for having us.
The amenities need updating 🙄

The caravan park has clean facilities and the best showers I’ve ever had at a holiday park. The location is great, right next to the water. Some spots are lovely, some are not!  We had an awful one assigned for our 8 day stay, really tight to get into (glad we had the purpleline mover on the van), backed on to the road, next to a permanent site van and no slab. We asked for another spot, with a slab, once the Easter crowds moved out and I negotiated the spot next to us which at least had a slab but then realised some of the much better spots were available for the rest of our stay which was really annoying as I wasn’t offered those and we were there for another 5 nights. So, ask for a slab (I had to pay extra when I moved -$6 for 5 nights???), ask to not be next to the road and ask to be where the tow bar is on a hill sloping back rather than forwards - we had real trouble getting the car hitched on as the tow bar was tipped so low and there was a broken drain and lip in the gutter. There is no way of opting out of their texts if you sign in online too - I got plagued with advertisements and meaningless announcements every day - sometimes 3-4 a day. Photos are first 3 day spot with no slab, new spot where caravan had to have 2 wheels on and 2 off slab as was too tight to fit down next to it, the sites that were vacant and would have been nice and the drain and hitching woes. The photos of the beach and water are a quick and easy walk away.
Great caravan. Very clean. Friendly staff. Highly recommended 👌
Caravan sites are adequate in sites, rest rooms are clean. Close to beach and swim areas. Short drive to inner city
Great location near the rock pool and beach.

Too many bad experiences to list. From check in, we had booked a month ago  - - - -  to Ants ,  cockroaches  and an overly crowded  non useable  lounge and dinning area . Reception was not very welcoming. Heads up

PROFITS OVER GUEST SAFETY AND ENJOYMENT!  The night we stayed, torrential rain hit. We live in the area and were only staying to come see some friends for the night. With all the flooding that’s been occurring in our area, we made contact earlier in the day and asked if we could postpone our booking to another time. We were rudely told we knew the conditions when we booked so NO!  The weather was so bad that not even an hour later the office was SMSing guests to warn them of the bad weather. We were a one night booking, it’s not like we were cancelling a two week holiday. We do this once or twice a year only to come see friends who travel to the area. Never again, we will not be back. I’m embarrassed at the lack of compassion shown from a local business in our beautiful area and that they care more about profits than they do their guests - Shame on them!!

Word's cannot describe reflections caravan Park
The staff where so friendly and helpful
Cabin we stayed in so clean and tidy
The van park was also  clean  and so close to all amenities including the shops
We loved so much we rebooked  it again
Once again a BIG thank you for a wonderful weekend away
See you next year
Great place for holidays. Close to shops cafes and beach.

My 5 stars to the guys who are managing this place, we had a very wonderful time staying. Amenities are top class and this was our best camp sites ever stayed. Kitchen is great here, class bathroom and very well maintained. Although we had to leave due to the heavy wind as our tents were almost in a flying situation, our stay was fantastic and we enjoyed every bit of it. Close to the shops, woollies is just a 4 min drive and this is located at a very central spot. This place is more secure than I thought. Keep up the good work.
Great location,  beautiful amenities, and friendly staff!!
Great location, shame there is no pool but all other facilities are fantastic including washing machines and dryer that send you a message when they are all done. Very happy with our stay

This park is in a great location close to the shops, restaurants, good walks and the beach but I wouldn’t recommend a premium waterfront site. Granted these sites have big double slabs which are great but they are also directly under huge pine trees and while they are beautiful to look at they aren’t so great being parked under as they (assisted by the cockatoos) drop pine nuts on your car and caravan continuously which sounds like hail and makes a terrible mess. The sites under the particularly large pines are also very dark. The premium sites also back onto the only road leading to the boat ramp and public carpark which appears to double as a meeting place for rev-heads, youths on electric bikes and scooters and hoons,  all of which use the road as a place to wheel spin and generally loudly show off especially at weekends. The sunsets over the water are beautiful but marred somewhat by the huge grey metal fence surrounding the park. The issues I raise are not the fault of the park. I’m just giving my opinion on the premium sites in particular as I was very disappointed by the one we were on.
Fantastic place to stay close to the water. Eco cabin was great, staff went above and beyond.

We stayed in one of the large static homes and the attention to detail was amazing.  It was clean, bright, in a lovely location, just a short walk to everything.
Would 100% recommend.

Unfortunately they have spoiled the park with the placement of cabins on all of the lower sites which now completely blocks any outlook you used to have of the river and gorgeous sunsets from caravan sites.  Completely changed the ambience of the park.  Still the location is great,  but it has gone down in our list of our "go to" parks.

Great location but that is where it ends. No children facilities except for a dump sand pile on the way in with a few sand buckets.
Beautiful new camp kitchen but no power points that can be used. The new amenities block is awesome but it’s always dirty and never cleaned.
Our site was like a mud pit. Every time it rained it was under 2 inches of water and the water would not drain away.
We ended up leaving 3 days early as the mud being walked through our caravan was horrible.
Trying to get in contact with reception is useless. Firstly told that the girl that looks after bookings is off sick. Called back the next day and she is now out. Still awaiting  call back. Tried to call this morning and the phone just rings out after around 1 minute then goes to voicemail mail.

We have stayed many times at different reflections caravan parks. This one has to be the worse one we have stayed at.

Thankyou to Lat and the house keeping team, very professional service and makes us feel at home every time.
Thank you to Ray as well for always being so accomodating. Our family stays here every Easter and we look forward to visiting our home away from home each time.
Fantastic staff, location and place. Definitely worth a visit.
Lovely spot, new toilets and showers are nice.
Well maintained spot. Walking distance to beach, shops and dolphin watching.
Great place to chill close to the main shopping street and beach

Staying in a standard cabin - one bedroom with bunks and a small bathroom. The CLEANEST cabin accommodation we have ever stayed in. On arrival the cabin smelled so CLEAN and everything was IMMACULATE. The pillows are hotel grade -  we brought our own from home but didn’t need them. Although it’s a small cabin there is plenty of floor space which makes it feel bigger. The fancy looking soap, shampoo, conditioner etc dispensers are a nice little touch.
The holiday park itself has nothing to offer for kids, only those pedal karts, and that is perhaps why this place is more affordable than other places with jumping pillows, splash park, pool etc.
The location is tops; direct access to the beach is a bonus.
Two free ev chargers onsite, which is amazing.
We would definitely stay here again.

Checked in around 12 pm. with our van. We have a concrete slab, which is fantastic due to heavy rain today, Saturday, and showers during the week.
Amenities: Very clean washing machines $7. per wash. Park is not very busy.
Park walking  distance to beach,coffee shop,supermarket, post office and club.

Reflections Tuncurry is a very well situated, up to date, holiday park, in a lovely area, close to all facilities. A great place to stay when you need a lovely time away from home. Thank you so much. Would highly recommend to all our family and friends 😃

This is our second time staying at the Tuncurry Park, returning after such an enjoyable stay the first time two years ago.
Sadly your staff have let you down this stay,with poor customer service (after all this is their core job description 😕)
We had booked two sites together as we were travelling together and were guaranteed sites together and paid our deposit. On arrival we were told one of the sites were given away and were separated one end of the park to the other, placed on a double slab and want to charge extra!!
The park was not at capacity and has not been during the duration of our stay.
We were after a 30 plus minute check in time, we were given two of the most difficult sites to manovure our size caravans on to.
As one of our travelling companions is terminaly ill and need more time to rest we extended our stay by an additional night. On booking this additional night we were charge and additional fee. Told this was for extending our stay. When I returned to the office I was told by the girl at the desk this was an additional fee because the park was at 75% capacity?? without even looking, I have never heard of such a fee??  When I questioned if I booked on line the fee would be different again ?? 🤔
So take from this, they charge what they want when they want, won't guarantee you the site you booked and paid for and offer no apologies.
Customer service at this park is officially dead.

Nice park. Grounds are well maintained. Check-in was easy.  Facilities are starting to look worn. Some sites can be tight to get into, but most are ok. 5 minute walk to the beach area. Enjoyed our stay and we would stay there again.

The 3 gates to the beach are now left open all night so no security anymore as used to be closed at sunset to stop people walking thru and stealing your goods. Noticed lots of people just walking through the park and checking out belongings left outside that weren't staying in park, not good.|When I asked Ed the manager he said these gates are emergency exits which isn't true at all, as I checked with WorkCover. New manger Ed is just to lazy to have the gates locked which secures your belongings as the people have to enter thru the main entry gate which have many security cameras.

We have visited this park 4 times
Enjoyed our visits. Contacted park direct 6/1/25 to enquire about disability cabin. Booked out for months. After 30 min phone call established deluxe cabin 4 style would suit as could transfer from wheelchair to bed. Could also manage gentle steps with 2 handrails with assistance. We were offered cabin 15 dark and had a strong odour . Back to office offered villa for extra $480 for 3 nights on top of what we had paid for deluxe cabin. Thankfully staff couldn't change charge rate. Thankyou. Thankfully we had accommodation as the place was packed. However access was via 4 steep steps with one handrail. My independence was gone needed to be manhandled in. Manhandled off the lounge as so low. Manhandled to bed. Am sore from being hauled around and needed someone to stay with me. A really disappointing holiday. Not sure we'll be back
Over priced for pensioners $72 per night..

Had 2 nights here had a nice cabin couldn t get past the tree blocking the entry
Had a problem with the code so mention it to a staff member passing by to which she replied go to the office then started to verbally critasise me under her breath but I was aware of wat she was doing it was so disturbing
That afternoon was told our second car was a problem in a very rude manner so we parked it outside the park in the street as did a friend of ours
Quite rude and for the money spent there very upsetting
We took our fur baby and had a wonderful weekend away,we are going back later this year.
Amazing location,  super friendly and helpful staff, great amenities, and good-sized camp sites.  Worth a visit and stay.

My Family and l camped.
This was the most disappointing holiday over the Xmas period l have experienced.
Toilets where discussing and shower facilities.
5 of the campers had to go to management for them to be cleaned.2 toilets and 2 showers for alot of sites.Thisdid not accommodate families
Showers where not the best either.
Our camping sites was a walk to the pool and water.
The pool did not accommodate the amount of people at the park.
We where camping on dirt and put in a paddock near the entrance to the main road travelling into the park. Which was dangerous for children with cars entering the park.
B.B.Q area was a tent with a portable B.BQ.
Was very disappointing for the money per night.
The sites are very small and you where packed in like sardines The laundry facilities where not working.
Sorry did not enjoy this stay at all.

Very disappointed with where we were placed in the caravan park…we were placed in amongst people who have been coming to the park for years everyone knew everyone and they definitely didn’t want to know the interlopers…we had our caravan opened to have the beautiful OCEAN breeze and I went inside of the van to get something and all I could smell was cigarette smoke(none smoker here) and I looked out of the bedroom window and here’s a bloke smoking right next to the open window I closed the window and said to the owner of the tent next to us could they Not smoke next to the caravan window he’s comment was I don’t smoke and I than asked if he wouldn’t mind asking he’s visitors to not smoke near open window…1 hour later I tried to apologise to him if I came across aggressive he was having none of it he became aggressive to me and wouldn’t accept my Apology and said that I was aggressive towards he than walked away…SO I’ll NEVER COME TO THE TUNCURRY REFLECTIONS CARAVAN PARK AGAIN…Never VERY DISAPPOINTING.

Great booking system, very friendly and helpful staff, beautifully maintained, new and modern facilities, perfect location minutes from beach, marina, shops in main street, surf club, great options for accommodation and very quiet. Highly recommended.

Great facilities and location.
Very tight packed sites though.  Had a new bike stolen overnight while locked to wheel of motorhome. And outboard was also stolen I believe from someone else.
The amount Reflections charges for everything it should be able to afford much better security.  I won't be staying there again until it's more secure.

we stayed in a deluxe cabin and our room was infested with ants and cockroaches, was super gross and scary. i hate cockroaches and we were there for 3 nights, saw a big roach each night and ants were in the kitchen, bathroom and bedrooms.
but overall the location is great and decent space for 5 people

Nice park in a great location. Original site allocated didn't suit our Motorhome as we could level the vehicle on the slope. Staff happily offer us alternate sites to choose from. The park is a little dated and pokey but it looks like they are making improvement. New amenities block was clean however already showing signs of wear and tear. Price is up there but would happily stay again.
Great caravan park
Took our camper for the week. Great spot. Close to everything. Amenities are clean as a whistle. Friendly helpful staff.

Forster never disappoints. Reflections has a fabulous location with most things within walking distance. Could not fault our stay at the holiday park in the fully accessible air conditioned cabin. Beautifully set out with enough room to park our car and boat. Spacious inside enabling our daughter to use her wheelchair. Bathroom fully accessible. Kitchen fully equipped. Justine is a great host and all the staff are very helpful and obliging. Thank you for a very enjoyable holiday.

This caravan park has the best location in Forster, nestled between the Marina, Beach and shops. Amenities are great and support staff are helpful. Unfortunately like many caravan sites it appears to be favoring cabin, which is likely a cash grab.
BAD SERVICE. After our booking they informed us that the campsite was just for a tent and not our campervan. NEVER GOT OUR MONEY BACK.
Nice caravan park close to the water.
Great park.

We stayed on a powered campsite for 1 night, the prices do not equal what you get (62 dollars for 1night). The showers where outdated, not fresh + weak shower hose. It's nice that it's so central if the town was worth while but sadly Forster felt like an ugly beach town on the Belgium coast 😭, nah its not that bad but definitely seen some more pritty beach towns like Coffs Harbour and so would recommend to spend your money elsewhere!
On a good note the staff where friendly 👍
A great park to stay at! Very close to town, great facilities and great staff.

G8 park, g8 place, g8 staff, g8 location. On 5th Sept 2024 we spent 1 night at the park on a powered site. I was quite shocked when I was charged $63 for a night's accommodation, I should've done my homework and rang around before I paid by credit card   because after contacting NRMA Tuncurry my accommodation wouldve been $45 per night including my  NRMA discount. We are members of Reflections rewards club and are loyal visitors to the caravan park since 1993  we thought the price $63 per night was extremely expensive and we will never use Forster Beach caravan park for an overnight stay as loyal users of the park we thought we were completely ripped off  now we know why the park was empty.

Great spot. NO Wifi for the TV if you want to watch a family movie. Only for your phone. After-hours contact is they leave an email message for the next day to address an issue. We had no gas in our BBQ had to wait the next day to use it. Will stay somewhere else next time. At $400 a night we should have had gas and access to wifi.

Ideal spot to base yourself in Tuncurry.

Close to the beach. Breakwall and shops.

An easy stroll to the bridge to walk across to Forster.

Importantly for us this park is dog friendly.
Tuncurry nice spot. Reflections caravan park amenities nice and clean. Close to the beach.

Great location.  One of the best locations on coast . Will return. van sites a bit small as are most older parks on coast. Well maintained  park .  Friendly helpful staff. Facilities  aged  but clean. Good children's playground equipment. Walking distance to shops.  Club and beach.
Great spot by the lake.

We return again and again to the Tuncurry Beach Reflections. Good and very clean amenities, We have a caravan each site has room for awning out and car next to our van. The staff are very friendly and lovely walks from park.  They had bbq areas, chairs around firepit, I presume they would only be available in off fire season.Close to a Club, shops and beach. Win all around. They also allow pets. Our golden retriever spent the week with us and many others had their pets. All were very responsible and I saw no mess or deposits from the dogs.
Always a great place to stay. A beautiful setting, central to everything.  We have visited with our caravan at least 12 times.
Great spot to stay a few day's,  very clean and friendly.
Lovely caravan park close to the beach and shops. Friendly staff. Will stay here again.

Park is a little old, however it is kept well.
Very close to the river mouth and beach.
Enjoyed our stay.

Hubby and I recently stayed in a basic cabin around $130 p/n cabin C3. We originally booked 4 nights but left before the 4th night due to the large amount of black mould throughout the cabin; on ceilings in bed area, ceiling living area, ceiling in the bathroom, it was on walls in patches and in corners where the walls meet the floors.

Yes I could have gone to the office and had a windge but doubt it would have made any difference because as we walked past other cabins of the same standard I looked through the windows to see the same black mould growing on ceilings as the one we were in.

In the future we will be going elsewhere.
Me and my dog travelling
The $135 cabins are very small & basic. Would not recommend. The best points are that the bed is reasonably comfortable and there appears to be plenty of hot water.
To expansive for 1 night
Great location friendly staff very clean premises

They don't know the meaning of the words customer service. Found them to be completely unreasonable and unprofessional in dealing with management regarding a refund. I will never stay at a reflections park again!
A great CP with good space a great location and lovely helpful service. Handy to beach, cafes, lookout and shops.

I can't ask for more, excellent views, very close to town and the marina. The cabins are spacious and well appointed. A beer on the verandah watching the sun set over the river, great way to end the day.
I stayed in a cabin. It's ok if you're there alone. The bathrooms are tiny. If you had 4 people it would be really cramped

A lovely park close to everything.  Spent 2 nights in one of the cabins.  The cabin had everything we needed for a lovely stay including a kitchen with ample equipment,  comfortable beds a 2 seater couch and a lovely outdoor seating area. The shower was lovely and hot but quite cramped, would not be comfortable for a large sized person.  It is possible to walk everywhere including into Tuncurry

Well maintained park, sites are generous with water, power and sullage. Great outlook from sites along marina. Close to shops and eateries. Sites over the back can be difficult to access if you have a low van. Amenities are well maintained.
Close to pubs &restaurants
Two of us stayed at Reflections Forster Beach Holiday Park whilst on a coastal road trip. Friendly staff, access to good ammenities, and well-maintained park. Thank you for our stay!

Have stayed at the park for many years, but getting disappointed by how run down the park is becoming. All the roads are in a bad state of disrepair and are very dangerous especially for elderly people walking around as there are large pot holes and loose gravel everywhere. The park is now looking very outdated compared to other parks in the area. The bins are only emptied a few times a week and the general waste bins were full of maggots. For the substantial increase in fees each year, you would expect a little more from the park.
Loved staying here, was only for one night but can’t wait to go back and spend more time in area. Friendly staff, very accommodating for our big van at short notice. Well kept park!
Standard cabin was very well equipped, comfy beds and pillows. Staff were accommodating
Great spot & perfect villa to compliment it
Nice park clean & tidy pet friendly off leash beach within walking distance 😎😎
Great place to stay. Close to rock pool for a swim, cafes, town 2 blocks away. Prices are quite competitive.

We love the location,close to shops, bowls and the rock pool.
Amenities are lovely and clean.

Honestly if it wasn't for the location of this park we would never stay.

We booked a slab powered site. We were allocated. Site A17 (I think) which was in between two onsite vans. The site was that narrow mainly due to the light post and power/water connections - you could not get your van beside the slab ( we have a small van)  therefore we had to park the van on the slab this is annoying for many reasons with a child. This site should be allocated to campers. Not vans.

Across from us was a very large group who had 3 sites allocated, there were 4 vehicles on one site with the other two sites having each  a car, boat & caravans . This made it extremely hard even getting our car in and out of our site. Understandably it was a long weekend so we did expect some riff raff however this was next level for a "family friendly park".
The profanities coming from these sites was atrocious. Not to mention the loud music and banter continued well past 11pm.
These sites also had numerous dogs which remained off leads at all times. We approached the occupants of the site only to be met with hostility.

There were no patrols from management whatsoever.

I did approach management asking if they could at least send a reminder about dogs are to be on leads..( this was also a large breed dog that charged onto our site with a small child) i was advised this would happen however the animals remained off leash for the remainder of our stay. Once again never saw any kind of patrol from management. I also advised that the group was rowdy and rude and the response I received was "oh you get that sometimes"

Facilities have not been upgraded for who knows how long.

This park could be excellent with some strict management and a good tidy up.
very close to beaches and nearby shops. very good to a holiday break.
Second time visiting , caravan site and cabins for the kids , amenities are very clean , walking distance to Tuncurry and close to beach and a stones throw to the breakwater pool ,
Price was mixed up.   Got one price had to ring back to confirm  and the price had changed almost $100more wasn't happy.
Love this park. Great sites. Close to the Rock Pool. Easy walk to the shops. Facilities nice and clean. Disabled amenities also.
Great cabins & camp sites close to the lake.
Solid holiday park right by the rock pools.

Loved the park. Will come back for sure. Marked the rooms down to 4 stars partly because we brought our own (caravan) but because the roads are more like laneways and are difficult to enter and exit at either end.  Spots are generous with three car widths each (one for can, a second for awning and a third to park car).

Service at front desk was lovely and helpful.  Park put on music and various other holiday time activities.

Location is excellent being right on the lake and beach.
Tight spots. Close to shops and beach.  Great location

Wir haben versehentlich im Reflections Bonny Hills und im Reflections Forster gebucht. Direkt nach dem administrativen Fehler haben wir angerufen, um unseren Fehler zu melden und nur eine Buchung zu stornieren. Dies wurde telefonisch bestätigt. Allerdings wurde die doppelte Buchung nie storniert und nachträglich erhielten wir lediglich einen Gutschein für den Preis (1 Jahr gültig)! Wir leben in der Schweiz und können keinen Gutschein gebrauchen... Nach mehrfachem Nachfragen habe ich lediglich die "Rules & Conditions" erhalten und schliesslich gar keine Antwort. Extrem schlechter Kundenservice und 0.0 Flexibilität beim Service.

Very disappointing. Paid extra for boat space and every time we went to park in our spot we paid for someone was parked in it. Tried to get staff to help but they just made it more difficult. Rooms are outdated over pricedand dirty
The actual powered site we were given was a real disappointment, uneven, unlevel, and overpriced for what we were given. Amenities very good as well as the location next to water.
Exc

Could have been cleaned better
Oil leak of some sort on bedside table
Smears on fridge and microwave
Noticed mould appearing in corners of rooms
Nice & relaxed

Always a great place to stay. Close to everything u need.
Staff are always willing to help

Very disappointing
Two couples staying in a cabin,  plenty of parking space yet only one car allowed in park.
Cabin is old and run down,  decking feels like it is going to collapse under you.
All kitchen utensils had to be washed as everything was filthy especially the glasses,  looked like a 2 year old had washed them.
Only thing going for the park is the closeness to the shops etc.
The big 4 in tuncurry is a much better place to stay,  only staying here as friends are here

I was told I couldn't have the site I wanted because the toilet block was closed and the council regulations had to be within so far from campsites. Why not just open the toilet block. Was will open it for school holidays. What was the big deal. I always thought look after the customer. Also every time we go there there is noise. Last time demolition was being performed and we had a big jack hammer all day going for our 2 day stay and this time was told after we set up camp and paid road works were being performed at the entrance round about for two nights. So we had no sleep for two nights. Why weren't we told on check in and offered a reduction in price. No they waited till 5pm by text to inform us.  I have been going there for years but  now every time you go there now there is a new rule change or there is noise all day or all night. I am seriously considering switching parks I Forster

As mentioned below, this place is overpriced for what you get. Nothing fun for the kids to do, especially in winter when you don't really go to the beach. No views of anything nice. The male park manager was rude, obnoxious and on his high horse.

Our camp site was so small, our camper trailer only just fit but we couldn't set the whole thing up. Our car had to almost be parked on the road.

Overnight we heard a massive bang, but thought it was a garbage truck. Come to find out my car had been smashed into by the park managers car, with a note left on my windscreen. At first I thought, accidents happen. Unfortunately there was no sorry, ever.. no sorry on the note, no sorry from the mangers, no sorry from the ladies in the office, no sorry from head office.
The first sorry I got was from MY insurance company. What a pain in the *** my last 3 months have been, since my stay in June.

I wouldn't recommend this park to anyone, do yourself a favour and try somewhere else in Forster!

And no need for staff to reply to my message now with any 'sorrys'.. too little too late.

I rented a cabin for the weekend with my wife. When we arrived, the receptionist greeted us warmly and directed us to our cabin. At first glance, the cabin was outdated and in need of repair, but because we were not booking a stay in a five-star hotel, we dismissed that problem. Instead, we left our luggage in the cabin and went out to supper. After returning from Dinner and a walk, I needed to warm up, so I turned on the water to start a shower. After soaping myself, the water turned ice cold, so I tried to turn it off, but the tap kept spinning and spinning until the handle fell off in my Hand.
angry, cold, and soap covered, I used a towel to dry off and realised that the only heater I had was a strip heater.   When we finally decided to go to bed, I pulled back the covers to discover that they had not been changed since they were filthy with dust and hair.  I requested a callback by sending an SMS to the specified number. Nobody did.
The next morning, I went into the office and started to tell my story to the receptionist, when the Manager Eddy holding a child mentioned he saw the SMS and asked why I did not call him???   I said that I was unsure if someone was awake and decided to do the right thing and SMS and wait for a response. After telling him what had happened, he said, "What do you want me to do about it?  it wasn't if we were being Malicious" I calmly and quickly explained that I understood that, yet I needed to resolve the issue. I asked him if he could move us to a cabin that worked and was clean, he said "there is none available."  Angry yet trying to keep my head, I walked out the door then turned around and said “I know what you can do you can credit me and I will find another accommodation place to stay he agreed.   It has been 8 Days now and still no credit.
My Belief is that the Manager Eddy Should Not be there he has Zero People Skills.
Not kids friendly park it is for the elderly will never take family back there management Justine was quite rude definitely not a family holiday spot won’t be going back
Definitely not a family oriented or kid friendly holiday park !!!

Awful. Uneven sites covered in cigarette butts, portable shower block main amenities closed up. Traffic noise terrible . Warnings on paperwork to lock everything up and secure bikes,kayaks etc ....certainly didn't have a good feel ...

The rooms are basic and quite dated, but they did the job. Apart from the shower’s water pressure, the mattress was definitely the highlight of the entire experience considering the positive aspects of our stay. Upon entering the room for the first time, there was a common, nostalgic aroma which took me back to a place that I was familiar with but couldn’t quite put my finger on… A rich, lemony freshness that my wife just could not share the same feeling for. It didn’t take me long to find the source of this intoxicating bouquet. Nestled on top of the wardrobe was a familiar yellow dorito which one could mistake for an exfoliating shower scrubber, but was in fact a urinal mat, brightening my weekend. Not to ‘take the piss’ too much though. It was a great location and we had fun for the couple of nights we were there.
This is a great spot. Close to the rockpool, beach and cafe. Lot's of options for day trips etc.
Best location especially if you surf. Facilities could do with some TLC.
Another great stay in one of our favourite places. Brilliant location, clean amenities, great customer service. Angie couldn't do enough for us. Thanks guys. See you next time!

Basic but clean. Note the sites near the trees have you looking at wire fencing with barbed wire! No information on gates or check in handouts about gate to beach getting locked at night - so no moon watching, sun rise watching or early/late fishing unless you want to drive or walk the long way out the front of the park.
No facilities or anything of interest for kids.
They also don’t warn you if you’re placed next to a large group - who were super noisy.

Excellent location
Great service
Great size park, plenty of sites,  just visiting, booked our sites for September  races, close to club, water and main street
Not happy with service and especially SAFETY

This park is in a great location right on the off leash beach, a short walk to the break wall and the rock pool cafe. Staff were very helpful and lovely upon check-in

The sites were nice and a reasonable size

I love that the reflections has a Dogwash

I was tossing up between staying here at the NRMA. The NRMA actually has discounted rates which reflections even when the park was practically empty was quite expensive at $50 a night. I chose here due to the off leash beach. Staying at the NRMA I could have saved about $40-$50 a night

It’s a huge park and is very nice but Some site would be very hard to get into a navigate in peak time I think

Great location
Friendly, informative staff
Tired amenities but wonderful staff
Great place to stay. Close to everything
Huge park, dog friendly, staff accommodating, easy access to shopping centre. Some sites are a bit small for the larger vans.

A big thank you to Bec, Alex and the team. Recently my parents house burnt down and as I live in an unsuitable location, I needed to source a safe place for them to reside. The team at Reflections were empathetic, helpful, kind and respectful. The accommodation was clean, warm and ample whilst we took time to find permanent accomodation.  A big thank you to you all and I would highly recommend the park for all your holiday needs.

Cheers Charne (Warren and Judy Musgrove)
Great view over water .

Great park , great location stayed in both top cabins on hill. 2nd cabin has a beautiful view, but can't see it as the hedge is grown up, Really need clipping down lower. A waste of a good view. If not clipped will have to consider going elsewhere.

Everything done via constant annoying txt messages
Didn't see one staff member all weekend .. cut tree with leaves and a branch poking out scratched the whole side of our brand new caravan ...limb was hidden by leaves ..sites are very cramped up the far end

What a great spot! Right in the middle of everything.
Staff were wonderful, amenities were tidy
Great location facilities were clean and well maintained

Dragging the old van up to Forster was worth it.
Lovely clean, well maintained sites, friendly staff, walking distance to town and fishing.
